codeReview.summary: The lifecycle feature implementation is complete and correct. All three commands (resolve, release, rm) are implemented according to spec with proper status transitions, reparenting behavior, JSON output support, and comprehensive integration tests. The sd-core additions (delete_tension, TensionDeleted event) are well-designed and properly tested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and followed the pattern of using Store methods for database operations and recording mutations automatically. The additional `release_reason` mutation recording in cmd_release follows the existing pattern but isn't explicitly documented as a convention in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/main.rs:691-702 - cmd_release records release_reason mutation manually after status update, similar to how other commands record field mutations

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the rust-cli-worker skill procedure exactly: checked preconditions, wrote tests first (RED), implemented (GREEN), ran lint/format, manual verification, and committed. No deviations were recorded.

The reparenting logic has subtle differences between commands that could benefit from documentation: resolve/release reparent to roots (null), while rm reparents to grandparent. This distinction is clear in the code but not documented in shared state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: sd-core/src/store.rs:1008-1114 - delete_tension reparents to grandparent; main.rs resolve/release use update_status which handles reparenting to roots via Forest/Store logic

summary: The foundation-lifecycle feature is well-implemented and passes review. All expected behaviors are covered: resolve/release/rm commands work correctly with status transitions, children reparenting (to roots for resolve/release, to grandparent for rm), --json output, ID prefix resolution, and proper mutation recording. The 22 integration tests provide comprehensive coverage of happy paths and error cases. The sd-core changes (delete_tension method, TensionDeleted event, public record_mutation) are appropriately scoped and tested with 8 additional unit tests. No blocking issues found.
